title: wedelia horsfieldiana
intro: Wedelia horsfieldiana, also known as Horsfield's Wedelia, is a tropical plant native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and characterized by its bright yellow flowers and lance-shaped leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Wedelia horsfieldiana include its potential to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory and gastrointestinal disorders. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and decoctions, and can also be used topically as a paste or cream to accelerate wound healing and reduce pain.
